original_body:
[ENH]: Add get/set_antialiased to Text objects
### Problem

Currently, Text objects always retrieve their antialiasing state via the global rcParams["text.antialias"], unlike other artists for which this can be configured on a per-artist basis via `set_antialiased` (and read via `set_antialiased`).

### Proposed solution

Add similar getters/setters on Text objects (also adjusting Annotations accordingly, if needed) and use that info in the drawing stage.

Should be relatively easy to implement, except that the slight fiddling needed with backends requires some understanding of backend code (I think we need to replace the access to `rcParams["text.antialiased"]` by going through the GraphicsContext state).
enhanced_title: Add get/set_antialiased methods to Text objects for per-artist
enhanced_body:
Problem Description                                                             

Currently, Text objects in matplotlib always retrieve their antialiasing state  
from the global rcParams["text.antialias"] setting, unlike other artists (like  
Line2D, Patch, etc.) which support per-artist antialiasing configuration through
set_antialiased() and get_antialiased() methods.                                

This inconsistency means:                                                       

 1 Text objects cannot have individual antialiasing settings                    
 2 Users must modify global rcParams to change text antialiasing, affecting all 
   text in the figure                                                           
 3 Text rendering behavior differs from other artists in the library            


Expected Behavior                                                               

Text objects should:                                                            

 1 Support set_antialiased() and get_antialiased() methods like other artists   
 2 Allow per-text antialiasing configuration                                    
 3 Respect the per-artist setting during rendering                              
 4 Maintain backward compatibility with existing code using global rcParams     


Current Behavior                                                                

Text objects:                                                                   

 1 Always use the global rcParams["text.antialias"] setting                     
 2 Have no way to override this on a per-text basis                             
 3 Require modification of global settings to change text rendering             


Reproduction Steps                                                              

                                                                                
@@ -1,2 +1,4 @@                                                                 
-# GitHub Issue                                                                 
+import matplotlib.pyplot as plt                                                
                                                                                
-Repository: matplotlib/matplotlib                                              
-Issue #25775                                                                   
+# Create a figure with default text antialiasing                               
+fig, ax = plt.subplots()                                                       
+ax.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Default antialiasing')                                      
                                                                                
-## Original Title                                                              
-[ENH]: Add get/set_antialiased to Text objects                                 
+# Try to set antialiasing on the text object (currently fails)                 
+text = ax.text(0.5, 0.5, 'Custom antialiasing')                                
+try:                                                                           
+    text.set_antialiased(False)  # This will raise AttributeError              
+except AttributeError as e:                                                    
+    print(f"Error: {e}")                                                       
                                                                                
-## Original Body                                                               
-[ENH]: Add get/set_antialiased to Text objects                                 
-### Problem                                                                    
-                                                                               
-Currently, Text objects always retrieve their antialiasing state via the global
rcParams["text.antialias"], unlike other artists for which this can be          
configured on a per-artist basis via `set_antialiased` (and read via            
`set_antialiased`).                                                             
-                                                                               
-### Proposed solution                                                          
-                                                                               
-Add similar getters/setters on Text objects (also adjusting Annotations        
accordingly, if needed) and use that info in the drawing stage.                 
-                                                                               
-Should be relatively easy to implement, except that the slight fiddling needed 
with backends requires some understanding of backend code (I think we need to   
replace the access to `rcParams["text.antialiased"]` by going through the       
GraphicsContext state).                                                         
-                                                                               
-                                                                               
-## Hints (files changed in fix)
